pattern: A full binary tree always has an odd node count, and removing the root splits the rest into two smaller full binary trees whose sizes must both be odd and sum to one less than the total.
hints: If the requested count is even, no full binary tree can have that many nodes at all.
Otherwise, the root consumes one node and the remaining nodes split into a left group and a right group, and both groups must themselves have odd size for either side to be full.
Recursively build every possible left shape and every possible right shape for each valid size split, then pair each left shape with each right shape under a fresh root.

edge_inputs.description: seven nodes, several distinct full binary tree shapes exist
three nodes, exactly one shape exists
smallest possible input, a single node with no children
an even count can never form a full binary tree
largest odd count allowed, exercising the full recursive growth
five nodes, a small but non-trivial shape count

stress.description: moderate odd sizes to keep total output volume bounded while still exercising recursion and memoization
occasionally hit a larger odd size closer to the constraint maximum
